intro: Checkout.com and Braintree both show up under the payment gateway category, but they solve slightly different jobs. Checkout.com publishes an entry price of custom pricing; Braintree does not publish one. Both sit at moderate lock-in. Transparency lands at 4/5 versus 5/5. Both tools target SaaS with online payments, so the choice comes down to pricing, lock-in, and operational fit rather than scope. Worth noting: Checkout.com is explicitly not for offline, Internal projects. The honest trade-off: Checkout.com trades off on offline-only retail; Braintree trades off on offline-only retail. On the plus side, Checkout.com highlights SaaS with online payments. Checkout.com's documentation also calls out e-commerce checkout. For teams evaluating payment gateway options today, the decision usually hinges on whether Checkout.com's profile or Braintree's profile maps more cleanly to the pricing model, lock-in tolerance, and scale your team can live with. Beyond the headline pitch, a documented Checkout.com use-case is "E-commerce checkout", and a documented Braintree use-case is "E-commerce checkout".
quick_take: Checkout.com is for SaaS with online payments; Braintree is for SaaS with online payments; decide on entry price.
verdict_a: Choose Checkout.com if your project is SaaS with online payments, usage-based billing at custom pricing matches your volume, medium lock-in is an acceptable trade-off.
verdict_b: Choose Braintree if your project is SaaS with online payments, medium lock-in is an acceptable trade-off.

faq.a: Checkout.com uses usage-based from custom pricing, and Braintree uses usage-based pricing. Checkout.com publishes an entry price of custom pricing, and Braintree does not, so a side-by-side cost comparison isn't possible from public data.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Checkout.com to Braintree?
faq.a: Our data puts Checkout.com at medium lock-in, and Braintree at medium lock-in (paypal-owned, card data portable). Migration is feasible but not trivial — budget time for re-integration, data export, and parallel running before cutover.
Both score 5/5 on developer experience in our data, so there's no clear winner on that axis. Braintree does edge ahead on pricing/docs transparency (5/5 vs 4/5), which can make evaluation faster.

faq.q: Is Braintree a good alternative to Checkout.com?
faq.a: Braintree covers a similar scope to Checkout.com — our data lists "SaaS, E-commerce, Marketplace" as the best-for on both. One caveat: Braintree is explicitly not for offline, Internal projects, so check that constraint against your use-case before switching.
